Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Assemblyman Bill Jones 
c/o District Office 
Administration

Executive/legal

Statements of Economic Interest 
322-5660 
322-5901 
322-6441 
322-6444 
April 10, 1985 
1441 So. Mooney Blvd., Suite "0" 
Visalia, CA 
93277 
Dear Assemblyman Jones: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-85-074 
You have written requesting advice regarding Chapter 1681, 
Statutes of 1984 (AS 2992 Waters) as it applies to members of 
city councils or boards of supervisors who act as members of the 
board of a Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO). 
Chapter 
1681 amended Government Code Section 84308 in several respects. 
As originally enacted Section 84308 applied only to 
"quasi-judicial boards or commissions" and did not include 
"legislative bodies such as city councils, county boards of 
supervisors," etc. 
The Fair Political Practices Commission 
considered the question of whether LAFCOs were covered by 
Section 84308 (Curiel Opinion, 8 FPPC Opinions 1, copy enclosed) 
and concluded that under the wording of Section 84308 they were 
not covered, because their decisions were quasi-legislative in 
nature rather than quasi-judicial. 
The Commission was 
dissatisfied with the policy implications of the conclusion it 
reached; however, it felt compelled to reach that conclusion 
based upon the law. 
The Commission introduced legislation (AS 2992 Waters) to 
make a series of changes in the law, among them to alter the 
coverage of Section 84308 so that it is no longer limited to 
quasi-judicial boards or commissions and instead now includes 


Assemblyman bill Jones 
April 10, 1985 
Page 2 
all agencies, with certain specified exceptions.ll LAFCOs do 
not fit within any of these exceptions and, hence, are covered 
as a result of Chapter 1681, see enclosed copy of regulation 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1. 
As a result of being covered by the amended version of 
Section 84308, a councilmember or supervisor who sits on a LAFCO 
board may become subject to the restrictions contained in 
Section 84308 regarding campaign contributions and campaign 
contributors. 
I trust that this letter has responded to your basic 
question regarding Chapter 1681. 
However, the specific 
application of these restrictions will depend upon the specific 
facts of a given situation and it is very difficult to provide 
further advice in the absence of concrete facts. 
If your 
constitutent desires further clarification, a further statement 
of the facts would be of great assistance. 
If you have further 
questions, please do not hesitate to calIon us. 
I may be 
reached at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
1~~w)~ 
Legal Division 
II Subsection (a) (3) of Section 84308 reads as follows: 
(a) 
The definitions set forth in this subdivision 
shall govern the interpretation of this section. 
* * * 
(3) 
"Agency" means an agency as defined in 
Section 82003 except that it does not include the 
courts or any agency in the judicial branch of 
government, local governmental agencies whose members 
are directly elected by the voters, the Legislature, 
the Board of Equalization, or constitutional 
officers. 
However, this section applies to any person 
who is a member of an exempted agency but is acting as 
a voting member of another agency.

A constituent has asked for information pertaining to the 
Government Code, Chapter 1681, as it pertains to members 
of the City Council and/or Board of Supervisors acting as 
members of the Board of LAFCO. 
Will you please advise my office regarding this matter. 
Is 
there a conflict in serving on the LAFCO Board if they have 
received contributions for their campaigns from LAFCO? 
Your attention to this matter is greatly appreciated. 
Sincerely, 
Y
